original_requirement: Build a simple task management app where users can add tasks and mark tasks as completed.

refined_requirement.description: Users can add tasks to manage their to-do items, with the ability to prioritize them as high, medium, or low, and store them in a general task pool with a limit of 10 tasks.

business_rules.text: The system allows users to add tasks to a general task pool.
Users can prioritize tasks as high, medium, or low.
The system stores added tasks in the task list.
The system limits the number of tasks that can be added to 10.
The task addition feature does not require a specific user task flow.

features.feature_summary: Users can add tasks to manage their to-do items, with the ability to prioritize them as high, medium, or low, and store them in a general task pool with a limit of 10 tasks.

user_stories.story: As a User, I want to add tasks to a general task pool so that I can manage my to-do items.

acceptance_criteria.text: The system allows the user to add a new task to the task pool.
The task is stored in the task list after addition.

user_stories.story: As a User, I want to prioritize tasks as high, medium, or low so that I can focus on the most important tasks first.

acceptance_criteria.text: The user can select a priority level (high, medium, or low) for each task.
The task priority is displayed in the task list.

user_stories.story: As a User, I want the system to limit the number of tasks to 10 so that I can avoid overwhelming the task pool.

acceptance_criteria.text: The system prevents the user from adding more than 10 tasks to the task pool.
An error message is displayed when the user tries to add more than 10 tasks.

epic.epic_summary: This epic delivers the ability for users to add tasks to manage their to-do items. It allows users to prioritize tasks and store them in a general task pool with a limit of 10 tasks.
epic.epic_description: The task management capability enables users to efficiently organize and prioritize their tasks, improving their productivity and time management skills. It provides a simple and intuitive way to add, store, and manage tasks, allowing users to focus on their most important tasks first.
epic.business_problem: The current way of working lacks a structured approach to task management, leading to inefficiencies and disorganization. Users struggle to prioritize and keep track of their tasks, resulting in missed deadlines and decreased productivity.

user_journey: Confirm task added to task list
gap_analysis: What happens when a user attempts to add an 11th task to the general task pool, given the 10-task limit?
How does the system handle task prioritization when a user tries to assign a priority level other than high, medium, or low?
Are there any specific error messages or notifications when a user exceeds the task limit or enters an invalid priority level?
Is there a mechanism for users to view or manage their existing tasks in the general task pool, or can they only add new tasks?
How does the system store and display tasks with the same priority level, such as multiple high-priority tasks?
Are there any constraints or rules governing the editing or deletion of tasks in the general task pool?
Can users add tasks with duplicate descriptions or details, or is there a check for uniqueness?
What is the behavior when a user tries to add a task with missing or incomplete information, such as no description or priority level?

user_stories.story: As a User, I want to add tasks to a general task pool so that I can manage my to-do items.

acceptance_criteria.text: The system allows the user to add a new task to the task pool.
The task is stored in the task list after addition.

user_stories.story: As a User, I want to prioritize tasks as high, medium, or low so that I can focus on the most important tasks first.

acceptance_criteria.text: The user can select a priority level (high, medium, or low) for each task.
The task priority is displayed in the task list.

user_stories.story: As a User, I want the system to limit the number of tasks to 10 so that I can avoid overwhelming the task pool.

acceptance_criteria.text: The system prevents the user from adding more than 10 tasks to the task pool.
An error message is displayed when the user tries to add more than 10 tasks.

clarification.task_list_constraints: No, to a general task pool

features.unit_text: users can mark tasks as completed

refined_requirement.description: Users can mark tasks as completed, which helps with task tracking and project management, and they will receive a notification with next steps.

business_rules.text: When a user marks a task as completed, it is removed from the task list.
Upon completing a task, the user will receive a notification with next steps.
A completed task can be reopened, but only with manager approval.
Completion of critical tasks requires validation.
The task completion feature is used for task tracking and project management.

features.feature_summary: Users can mark tasks as completed, which helps with task tracking and project management, and they will receive a notification with next steps.

user_stories.story: As a User, I want to mark tasks as completed so that I can track my progress and receive next steps.

acceptance_criteria.text: The task is removed from the task list after being marked as completed.
A notification with next steps is sent to the user after completing a task.

user_stories.story: As a User, I want to reopen a completed task so that I can make changes or updates as needed.

acceptance_criteria.text: A completed task can be reopened with manager approval.

user_stories.story: As a User, I want critical tasks to require validation upon completion so that I can ensure accuracy and quality.

acceptance_criteria.text: Completion of critical tasks requires validation.

epic.epic_summary: Users can mark tasks as completed and receive notifications with next steps, enhancing task tracking and project management.
epic.epic_description: The task completion feature allows users to effectively track and manage their tasks by marking them as completed, which in turn triggers notifications providing guidance on subsequent steps, thereby streamlining project management processes.
epic.business_problem: Inefficient task tracking and project management due to lack of clear completion status and follow-up guidance.
goals_and_objectives: Streamlined workflow with clear next steps

user_journey: Optionally reopen completed task with manager approval
gap_analysis: What is the definition of 'next steps' in the notification sent to users upon completing a task?
How does the system determine which tasks are considered 'critical' and require validation upon completion?
What is the process for manager approval when a user attempts to reopen a completed task?
How does the task completion feature handle tasks that are dependent on other tasks or have prerequisites?
What is the notification mechanism for users when a task is reopened, and how does it differ from the initial completion notification?
What are the specific requirements for task tracking and project management that the task completion feature is intended to support?
How does the system ensure that completed tasks are properly removed from the task list, as per business rule BR1?
What is the user experience for completing tasks on different devices or platforms, and are there any specific considerations for accessibility or usability?
How does the system handle errors or exceptions when a user attempts to complete a task, such as network errors or validation failures?

user_stories.story: As a User, I want to mark tasks as completed so that I can track my progress and receive next steps.

acceptance_criteria.text: The task is removed from the task list after being marked as completed.
A notification with next steps is sent to the user after completing a task.

user_stories.story: As a User, I want to reopen a completed task so that I can make changes or updates as needed.

acceptance_criteria.text: A completed task can be reopened with manager approval.

user_stories.story: As a User, I want critical tasks to require validation upon completion so that I can ensure accuracy and quality.

acceptance_criteria.text: Completion of critical tasks requires validation.

clarification.task_completion_status: It is removed from the task list
clarification.user_notification: Yes, a notification with next steps
clarification.task_reopening: Yes, but only with manager approval
clarification.completion_validation: Yes, but only for critical tasks
